title: Apply P-006+P-008a (WB): lock_busy contention events + path normalization at the log boundary
description: Edit .harness/bin/lock.py, .harness/bin/recontext.py, .harness/bin/notify.py. (1) P-008a: normalize every path-like field at each log_event call site: str(Path(p).resolve().relative_to(ROOT)) with try/except ValueError fallback to the original string (out-of-repo paths log verbatim, documented). Sites: lock.py:66/:98 (args.path), recontext.py EVIDENCE_PATH logs (~:58/:84/:105), notify.py:200 str(CONFIG_PATH). (2) P-006: add hc.log_event('lock_busy', ...) with normalized path + holder + requester + task on the busy-refusal branches of lock.py (~:50-60) and recontext.py (~:68-78). Owned files: those three. Acceptance: acquire with an ABSOLUTE in-repo path logs relative path (grep new event, no /Users/); a two-holder contention repro emits >=1 lock_busy event; out-of-repo path logs verbatim without crash; all CLIs still run clean. Handoff with replayable repro commands.

notes.note: plan: P-008a path normalization at log_event call sites in lock.py/recontext.py/notify.py via local per-file helper (no harness_common changes); P-006 lock_busy events on busy-refusal branches of lock.py + recontext.py

EXPECTED: P-008a normalizes every path-like log_event field to repo-relative (try str(Path(p).resolve().relative_to(hc.ROOT)) except ValueError -> verbatim), via a 4-line helper _normalized_log_path() duplicated identically in lock.py/recontext.py/notify.py (harness_common untouched, per partition). P-006 emits hc.log_event('lock_busy', path=<normalized>, holder=<current>, requester=<refused id>, task=...) on lock.py acquire's busy branch (~line 61-70) and recontext.py _acquire_evidence_lock's busy branch (~line 79-87), before the existing print/return, exit codes unchanged. ACTUAL: matches expected in both files; notify.py normalized its single site (notify_config_initialized path=CONFIG_PATH). All 3 files py_compile clean. Measurables run and observed: (a) absolute in-repo scratch acquire -> events.jsonl line has path=".harness/tasks/.scratch_T014_measurable_a.txt" (no /Users/). (b) 2nd acquire attempt as different holder on same path -> exit 1, lock_busy event logged with holder=substrate-worker-2, requester=substrate-worker-Y, task=T-014-contention-test, path relative. (c) out-of-repo /tmp path -> lock_name_for returns None so acquire refuses BEFORE any log_event (existing pre-T-014 behavior, unchanged) -- exit 1, 'refused: outside workspace root', no file created, no stray event; separately confirmed the new helper itself falls back to the verbatim string with no crash when called directly on an out-of-repo path. (d) recontext.py add (task T-014, scratch evidence line) -> lock_acquired/lock_released events show path=.harness/recontext_evidence.md (relative); notify.py init tested against a monkeypatched CONFIG_PATH scratch file (real notify_config.json left untouched since it may hold real settings) -> notify_config_initialized event path=.harness/tasks/.scratch_T014_notify_init_test.json (relative), scratch file deleted after. Regression: lock.py status/sweep/acquire/refresh/release all clean; recontext.py --help clean; notify.py status clean. All scratch locks/files cleaned up (verified via ls .harness/locks | grep scratch -> empty).

HANDOFF -> verifier: Replay commands (run from repo root):
1) Syntax: python3 -c "import py_compile; [py_compile.compile(f, doraise=True) for f in ['.harness/bin/lock.py','.harness/bin/recontext.py','.harness/bin/notify.py']]" -> expect no output (compiles clean).
2) Measurable (a) relative path on normal acquire: SCR="$(pwd)/.harness/tasks/.scratch_verify_a.txt"; python3 .harness/bin/lock.py acquire "$SCR" --holder verifier --task T-014 --ttl 30; tail -n1 .harness/logs/events.jsonl -> expect event lock_acquired with path=".harness/tasks/.scratch_verify_a.txt" (no /Users/). Then: python3 .harness/bin/lock.py release "$SCR" --holder verifier.
3) Measurable (b) contention: python3 .harness/bin/lock.py acquire "$SCR" --holder holderX --task T-014 --ttl 30; python3 .harness/bin/lock.py acquire "$SCR" --holder holderY --task T-014-b; echo exit=$?; grep '"event": "lock_busy"' .harness/logs/events.jsonl | tail -n1 -> expect exit=1 and a lock_busy event with holder=holderX requester=holderY path relative. Cleanup: python3 .harness/bin/lock.py release "$SCR" --holder holderX.
4) Measurable (c) out-of-repo: python3 .harness/bin/lock.py acquire /tmp/verify-not-in-repo.txt --holder verifier; echo exit=$?; ls /tmp/verify-not-in-repo.txt -> expect exit=1, 'refused: outside workspace root' message, no file created (existing lock_name_for()==None gate fires before any log_event -- unchanged pre-T-014 behavior). Direct helper check: cd .harness/bin && python3 -c "import lock; print(lock._normalized_log_path('/tmp/x')); print(lock._normalized_log_path('.harness/bin/lock.py'))" -> expect verbatim '/tmp/x' then relative '.harness/bin/lock.py'.
5) Measurable (d) recontext + notify still log relative paths: echo hi | python3 .harness/bin/recontext.py add --task T-014 --source verify:1 --label verify --agent verifier; grep lock_acquired .harness/logs/events.jsonl | tail -n1 -> path=.harness/recontext_evidence.md. python3 .harness/bin/notify.py status -> prints config normally (unchanged UX).
6) Regression: python3 .harness/bin/lock.py status; python3 .harness/bin/lock.py sweep; python3 .harness/bin/recontext.py --help; python3 .harness/bin/notify.py status -> all exit 0, no crashes.
All of the above were run by substrate-worker-2 during implementation with matching observed output (see task note). Files touched: .harness/bin/lock.py, .harness/bin/recontext.py, .harness/bin/notify.py only -- harness_common.py NOT touched (inline duplicated 6-line _normalized_log_path() helper in each of the 3 files per the disjoint-partition instruction). Please verify then set status done or kick back with specifics.

VERIFIED (replayed independently with fresh scratch paths, cognitive-diversity via direct helper calls + events introspection): P-006+P-008a. DIFF replayed: lock.py/recontext.py/notify.py each add local _normalized_log_path() (grep confirms helper in exactly those 3 files, count=0 in harness_common.py). Four measurables, my own runs: (a) acquire ABS in-repo path /Users/.../vrf_T014_a.txt -> new event path='.harness/scratch/vrf_T014_a.txt', grep /Users/ = 0. (b) two-holder contention -> lock_busy event {path relative, holder=vrf-holderX, requester=vrf-holderY, task=T-014-y}, busy refusal exit=1. (c) out-of-repo /tmp path -> 'refused: outside workspace root' exit 1, NO crash, NO stray event, NO file; direct helpers fall back verbatim ('/tmp/x') for out-of-repo and relativize in-repo abs ('.harness/bin/lock.py'). (d) recontext add -> lock_acquired/lock_released path='.harness/recontext_evidence.md' (relative). Exit code on busy branch unchanged (1). User-facing text unchanged: busy msg still prints caller path, notify status still shows abs config path, recontext --help intact -- only LOG events normalized. Regression: lock status/sweep, recontext --help, notify status all exit 0. Discipline: worker-2 locked all 3 files 07:12:11 before edit, handoff 07:16:21 (not self-done), released 07:16:27; no sibling files. Measurable-gains P-006 (grep -c lock_busy>0, now=3) and P-008 (abs-in-repo logs relative, 0 new /Users/ leaks) = TRUE. All scratch locks/files cleaned.
